title: GitHub repo/org listing API (backend)
prompt:
Add a backend capability to list the GitHub orgs and repositories the runtime's logged-in account can access, for the new 'add project by picking a repo' flow (vault requirement 81a8d).

Scope:
- Reuse the existing machine-local GitHub token from the github-auth service (src/github-auth/, ~/.kanban/settings/github-auth.json). Do NOT add a second auth mechanism.
- Add a tRPC endpoint(s) on the existing 'github' router (sibling of runtime) to: (a) list the authenticated user's orgs, (b) list repos the user can access (paginated/searchable), e.g. via GitHub REST API /user/orgs, /user/repos?affiliation=owner,collaborator,organization_member, /orgs/{org}/repos. Return enough per-repo to drive a picker + clone (full_name, clone HTTPS URL, private flag, default branch, description).
- Route all outbound calls through the runtime's proxy-aware fetch (globalThis.fetch is already patched) and honor existing logging conventions.
- IMPORTANT: verify the device-flow OAuth scope actually permits listing PRIVATE repos. The current client id is the GitHub CLI well-known device-flow id. If the granted scope is insufficient for private repos, document what scope is needed and surface a clear, typed error from the endpoint (do not silently return only public repos). Note findings in the task summary.
- Handle not-authenticated cleanly: return a typed 'not authenticated' state the UI can route to Settings, not a raw error.
- Add tests under test/runtime/github-auth/ for the listing/pagination/error mapping logic (mock the GitHub API).

Do NOT build the frontend picker here — that is a separate linked task. This task is the API + scope verification only.
